market_summary: Mixed-to-risk-on regime for the 30-day horizon: SPY +0.68% 1m, QQQ +0.53% 1m, and IWM +3.58% 1m, with all three above MA50 and MA200; breadth improved versus prior fragile readings, with small caps now leading.

Current book remains broadly aligned with leadership: KLAC, TSM, XOM, CVX, and GOOGL retain the strongest pack-derived momentum; COST remains the stabilizer, while NVDA is improving but still lags on 1m/3m relative strength and LMT has near-term softness despite solid medium-term trend.

No-go for an off-cycle rebalance today: cadence is not due, spacing discipline is binding, and the limited qualitative refresh did not verify severe thesis damage sufficient to override weekly turnover control.

thesis_damage_flags.why: Pack shows softer near-term trend than other core winners (1m -0.46%, 3m -2.74%) despite remaining above MA50/MA200; valuation remains rich at ~38x P/E and ~21.1x sales, so upside still depends on flawless execution. No fresh verified 7-day thesis-damage headline was available in the refresh.

Pack shows 1m weakness (-4.20%) and below-MA50 status, indicating some near-term loss of trend support. Fundamentals remain solid, but leverage is elevated (debt/equity ~7.9) and no fresh verified 7-day catalyst was available in the refresh.

No severe damage, but fundamentals are no longer accelerating: revenue growth -0.3%, earnings growth -3.7%, FCF growth -0.7% TTM. Momentum remains strong, so this is a watch item rather than an action trigger.

No fresh verified 7-day thesis-damage headline in the refresh; however some key ratio fields were not verifiable/complete, so fundamental confidence is modestly reduced to UNKNOWN on several growth/valuation subfields.

thesis: Catalyst: no verified 24-72 hour equity news shock or guidance change in the refreshed subset strong enough to justify breaking weekly spacing; current leadership in semicap and energy remains intact.
Factor edge: the existing portfolio still leans into higher-scoring momentum/quality pockets from the pack, led by KLAC, TSM, XOM, CVX, and GOOGL, while COST dampens portfolio volatility.

Variant view: improved benchmark breadth raises the odds that laggards like NVDA can recover without paying unnecessary turnover today; if that recovery fails by the next checkpoint, the replacement case can strengthen.
risks: Macro: a rates or growth scare could reverse the nascent breadth improvement and hit semicap/AI cyclicals disproportionately.
Idiosyncratic: NVDA and LMT remain the clearest watch-list names given softer near-term trend support; XOM/CVX remain exposed to commodity and geopolitical shocks.
Execution/liquidity: low for these large caps, but off-cycle trading still risks paying spread/slippage without a verified edge over a 30-day horizon.
falsifiable_checks: By the next weekly checkpoint, if NVDA underperforms both SPY and QQQ again on the 1m/3m windows or loses MA50/MA200 support, thesis damage escalates and replacement odds rise.

By the next weekly checkpoint, if LMT remains below MA50 and its 1m trend stays negative while higher-scoring alternatives retain above-MA50/MA200 structure, its seat in the portfolio weakens materially.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base: improved but still selective breadth means the expected benefit from trading today is modest, while turnover drag is certain. Bull: current winners in semicap and energy continue to carry the book and NVDA may catch up as growth leadership broadens. Bear: if breadth improvement fails and cyclicals roll over, preserving sizing discipline today still dominates because no severe verified thesis break has emerged. The expected edge does not clearly clear trading-cost drag before the scheduled rebalance window.

constraints_check.notes: Appendix: factor highlights—KLAC/TSM/XOM strongest momentum; COST best defensive risk stats; NVDA and LMT weakest near-term trend among holdings. Fundamental refresh—NVDA very high quality but expensive; XOM solid cash generation with decelerating growth; LMT solid FCF/earnings growth but leveraged; GOOGL fields partly UNKNOWN. Risk budget—Energy 30%, IT 38%, no crypto, no position >16%. Scenario stress—equity index shock hurts NVDA/TSM/KLAC/GOOGL most; oil shock hits XOM/CVX; rates shock modestly pressures long-duration tech; defense/geopolitical bid helps LMT; COST buffers broad risk-off. Execution plan—no trade today, reassess at weekly checkpoint. Monitoring/kill-switch—consider action if NVDA loses MA50/MA200 or if XOM/CVX trend support breaks; treat unverified qualitative fields as UNKNOWN rather than override pack data.
